content.full_text:
October 18, 1990

Mr. Scott Hallabrin  
Legal Division  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, 8th Floor  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for an Opinion involving Municipal fees to pay city costs to process land use applications

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I am requesting an opinion regarding the ability of a city to charge an applicant the costs incurred by that city to process land use applications. Our firm represents the City of Isleton as Deputy City Attorney. Specifically, in the City of Isleton, a group of developers is seeking annexation to the City as well as land use modifications. The City has only four staff members other than safety personnel and will have to rely on outside consultants to a great extent. The City proposes to charge the developers the actual amount necessary to process their applications which includes the cost of outside consultants, city staff time and application fees charged by third parties, such as LAFCo.

We are aware that the City of Folsom used "donation agreements" in some form which prompted an investigation by the FPPC. We would like some guidelines as to how to implement a program using developer contributions without running afoul of FPPC policies. In Isleton, the developers' money would be deposited in a City account and drawn down as expenses occurred. The developers would have no authority to hire, fire, or otherwise control the actions or recommendations of the consultants or staff.

It is possible that a series of development fees would be imposed on the developers if expenses were more than originally anticipated.

I am available to answer any questions you may have.

Very Truly Yours,

HYDE, MILLER & SAVAGE
A Professional Corporation

By: Lee Savage

LS/tdb
